center.name: 雨林
center.summary: 雨林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联99种鸟类，包括叢塚雉、鳳冠孔雀雉、Amboyna cuckoo-dove等99种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: African harrier-hawks are a common raptorial species south of the Sahara being most commonly found in the tropical regions of western Africa becoming less common in East and South Africa. African harrier-hawks are adaptable in their habitat preferences, occupying the following habitats in the Dzanga-Sangha Special Reserve in the Central African Republic: thick rainforest, forest edge, riparian areas, agricultural land and human occupied areas.{{Cite journal|last1=Keys|first1=G

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Amboyna cuckoo-dove inhabits rainforest, woodland, scrubland and rainforest regrowth areas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |date=2011-03-01 |title=The distribution of three nocturnal bird species across a suburban-forest gradient |url=https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1071/MU09117 |journal=Emu - Austral Ornithology |language=EN |doi=10.1071/MU09117}} In Australia they can be found in suburban and urban-fringe environments. Rivers and waterways are important to this species; a study in Australia found that they were more likely to be found in areas with waterways than those without. They are found in a variety of habitats such as open woodland, mallee scrub, tropical woodlands, and occasionally mangroves, open grasslands, and rainforests.{{Cite journal |last=Holyoak |first=David T

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It primarily inhabits the interior canopy of humid rainforest but also occurs at its edges, in tall secondary forest, and in shady semi-open woodland

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It primarily inhabits the interior of humid montane forest and foothills rainforest but is also found on forest edges and in clearings with scattered trees

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It inhabits monsoonal rainforests, subtropical, tropical forests, and wooded hillsides

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The species almost entirely inhabits the understorey of very humid terra firme primary rainforest, though it has also been recorded in várzea

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It can also be found throughout the Duke of York Islands, New Hanover Island, Manne Island, Selapiu Island, Tsoilaunung Island, Patio Island, and other surrounding smaller islands. It is found in lowland rainforest at altitudes of up to 500 meters, but prefers to live in areas less than 200 meters in elevation

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The black-and-white owl is mostly found in gallery forests and rainforest, but is also found in wet deciduous and mangrove forests, usually at an altitude between sea level and 2400 meters. Small ponds are also often visited by this species when hunting

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The black-breasted buttonquail is usually found in rainforests, foraging on the ground for invertebrates in large areas of thick leaf litter

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It inhabits humid lowland rainforest and secondary forest from sea level to

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In Venezuela and French Guiana it inhabits rainforest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |date=2020 |title=Blue-moustached Bee-eater (Merops mentalis) |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/historic/hbw/bumbee1/1.0/introduction |journal=In Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive |doi=}} Both sexes are generally similar in appearance, with predominantly purplish-blue plumage, a rich chestnut mantle, back, and wings. The species is native to the Upper Guinean forests, the Western High Plateau and Bioko, and occurs in Cameroon, Ivory Coast, Equatorial Guinea, Ghana, Guinea, Liberia, Nigeria, and Sierra Leone, where it inhabits submontane and upland rainforest habitats.{{Cite journal |last1=Sáfián |first1=Szabolcs |last2=Pyrcz |first2=D Tomasz W

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is the only hornbill species native to New Guinea, and one of the largest flying birds of the region. There have also been rare reported sightings on the Saibai and Boigu Islands in the Torres Strait, Queensland, Australia. Its diet consists mainly of fruits, especially figs, occasionally supplemented with insects and other small animals. The Papuan hornbill nests in a large tree hollow in the rainforest, from at least up to above the ground

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in subtropical or tropical moist upland forests and subtropical or tropical rainforests, usually above 300 meters

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It inhabits the interior and edges of rainforest and transitional forest, stunted forest on white-sand and other nutrient-poor soils, and the edges of terra firme forest

birds.evidence: In the Pacific, it mainly inhabits rainforest

birds.evidence: They are found in dry forests with dense undergrowth, rainforests, shrublands, coastal dune thickets, and in rushes and bracken along rivers and creeks.Morcombe, Michael (2012) Field Guide to Australian Birds

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It inhabits subtropical and tropical rainforest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is commonly found in most habitats on Christmas Island, including rainforest and gardens

birds.evidence: In French Guiana and Suriname it occurs in primary rainforest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Although found in many habitats, the cryptic warbler prefers evergreen, humid rain forest between elevation. It can be found in the canopy and sub-canopy of trees and shrubs between off the ground, with a diet consisting of insects. Its breeding season lasts from October to December

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It inhabits rainforest, mostly in lowlands but as high as in elevation

birds.evidence: It inhabits the floor of tall mature terra firme rainforest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It mainly inhabits open forest, gallery forest, and secondary growth, but is also found in mature rainforest with sparse undergrowth and tall trees

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: exilis, east central Brazil from Bahia south to Espírito Santo The golden-spangled piculet inhabits a variety of wooded landscapes including rain-, cloud-, and secondary forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: chrysosema, along the Madeira River and its tributaries from northeastern Bolivia into western Brazil's Mato Grosso state In most of its range the golden-winged parakeet inhabits undisturbed lowland rainforest, secondary forest, savanna, coastal woodlands, and towns (as long as there are tall trees)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It most commonly inhabits rainforests or forest edges of Indonesia and Papua New Guinea. They can also be found inhabiting mountainous habitats of the forests in New Guinea

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Green racket-tails can be found in the canopy of tropical lowland rainforests in elevations of up to where it mainly prefers primary forest but is also seen in secondary forest and forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This bird inhabits primary and secondary evergreen rainforests, almost always in the lowland but may inhabit foothills of up to high

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In much of its range it occurs in lowland rainforest and humid montane forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Luzon scops owl is known to be found in rainforest, montane forest, and tropical pine forest habitats at altitudes generally ranging anywhere from 350 to 1,800 m, but it may also be found at heights upwards of 2,200m. Although its habitat range across Luzon appears to be relatively large, it is noted to be uncommon and has a patchy distribution that corresponds to its preferred, highland habitats. It has been recorded primarily in northern Luzon provinces such as Ilocos Norte, Benguet, Cagayan, Isabela, Nueva Ecija, or near Manila in Bulacan and Quezon

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The species is rare and is listed as vulnerable in Queensland and occurs in subtropical rainforest and vineforest at altitudes from 50–800 m.Smith, G.C., Hamley, B.J., Lees, N., 1998

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The bird's plumage is unstreaked, with olive-brown uppersides (including the crown and wings) and creamy white undersides. The New Caledonia grassbird typically inhabits scrubby areas in the lowlands and hills of New Caledonia, particularly maquis minier with ferns, but also secondary forest and grasslands, and has even been seen in dense rainforest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It mainly inhabits the native subtropical rainforest, with lower densities in other wooded habitats

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It inhabits the floor of tall mature terra firme rainforest

birds.evidence: plumbea – eastern Paraguay and southeastern Brazil The plumbeous pigeon inhabits tropical and subtropical rainforest and cloudforest, both primary and secondary

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In the dry season it inhabits very wet and cool evergreen montane forest at elevations up to ; in the wet season it moves to lower elevation rainforest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: They are found in tropical rainforests and dense vegetation close to permanent wetlands

birds.evidence: hypospodius, eastern slope of Colombia's Eastern Andes The species inhabits rainforest and cloudforest, preferring thickets, second growth, and edges to the forest interior

birds.evidence: It is mainly found in rainforests and the margins of rainforests

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Elsewhere it occurs in lowland rainforest favoring várzea, but also savanna, remaining woodlots in otherwise cleared landscapes, and isolated mountains in the Guianan lowlands. In Brazil it locally reaches

birds.evidence: It mostly inhabits lowland rainforest, favoring undisturbed areas, but also occurs in mature secondary forest and sometimes in rather open landscapes

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The species inhabits dense growth in rainforest, typically around treefalls, road edges, and similar openings

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This bird is an endemic resident breeder found in south western rainforests of the island of Sri Lanka

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is commonly found in tropical dry rainforests or tropical moist lowlands throughout Central America. First scientifically described in 1860, it was originally thought to be the same as Platyrinchus mystaceus but was later reclassified as a sympatric species

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: right|thumb|Male at Burgers' Zoo, Netherlands Native to Australasia, the superb fruit dove lives in the rainforests of New Guinea, Australia, Solomon Islands, the Philippines and Sulawesi of Indonesia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It inhabits humid rainforest in the lowlands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: duidae: several tepuis in Amazonas state The tepui antpitta inhabits dense pre-montane rainforest and cloudforest on the slopes of tepuis, and on their summits more open stunted forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: minor: east of the Andes in Peru south of the Maranon and Amazon, south into Bolivia's Pando and La Paz departments, and east into western Brazil to the Purus River and perhaps beyond The demarcations between the ranges of the subspecies, and especially that between dissors and signata, are not well defined. The thrush-like antpitta inhabits humid rainforest in the lowlands and foothills, primarily terra firme

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Vagrants have been recorded from Senegal, and records from The Gambia and Liberia probably are also mostly or all stray birds; a supposed vagrant from Bioko is not anymore believed to have been of this species. It is not found in decidedly arid regions and dense forests such as the equatorial rain forest, but occurs on mountains up to 3,000 m ASL

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: latifasciatus, southeastern Peru, southwestern Brazil, and northern Bolivia The waved woodpecker primarily inhabits a variety of wooded landscapes including the interior and edges of rainforest, terra firme and várzea forest, secondary forest, and treed savannah

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: cuneatus, coastal eastern Brazil between the states of Bahia and Espírito Santo The wedge-billed woodcreeper primarily inhabits tropical evergreen forest including rainforest and to a lesser extent the lower reaches of montane evergreen forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The species inhabits a variety of wooded landscapes including rainforest, gallery forest, deciduous forest, mangroves, and open woodland

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: morphoeus, coastal eastern Brazil from Bahia south to Rio de Janeiro state The white-fronted nunbird inhabits a variety of wooded landscapes including lowland terra firme, rainforest, and gallery and transitional forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: They prefer to nest and roost in dry sclerophyll woodlands with sparse and discontinuous understory. Many individuals have been found to specifically live in woodlands that have experienced disturbances such as forest fires. In New South Wales, birds have been found inhabiting the edges of rainforests and mangroves. Those that winter in New Guinea occur in savannas, open grasslands, forest edges, marshlands and gardens

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: There it inhabits the canopy of mature terra firme rainforest, including forest on white-sand soils

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It may be found in rainforest, monsoon forest and along forest edges

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The yellow-breasted boatbill has a wider distribution across the entire island of New Guinea but can be found in higher population densities within North Queensland rainforests. Across all the listed geographical regions, the yellow-breasted boatbill is most commonly found in rainforest areas, where there is a high density of vegetation, typically composed of evergreen trees and shrubbery

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The yellow-casqued hornbill (Ceratogymna elata), also known as the yellow-casqued wattled hornbill, is found in the rainforest of coastal regions of West Africa, for example in Côte d'Ivoire

birds.evidence: chrysogaster]] of South America. It occurs mostly in trees in forest, woodland, and edge, but is generally not found in dense rain or cloud forests

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: sulphurescens: from eastern Minas Gerais and Espírito Santo states in southeastern Brazil south through eastern Paraguay into northeastern Argentina's Misiones Province and slightly into far northern Uruguay The yellow-olive flatbill inhabits a wide variety of forest landscapes including rainforest, montane forest, deciduous and semi-deciduous woodlands, secondary forest, and gallery forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It inhabits most commonly in rain forests or forest edges of Indonesia and Papua New Guinea. They can also be found inhabiting mountainous habitats of the forests in New Guinea

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Australian brushturkey inhabits rainforests and wet sclerophyll forests, as well as drier scrubs and open areas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |last=Duncan |year=2003 |publisher=Firefly Books |location=Richmond Hill, Ontario |isbn=1-55297-845-1 |page=33}} Though generally found in lowland rainforest or gallery forest in lowland savanna, the Papuan hawk-owl is occasionally found at elevations of up to above sea level. It is probably a resident species in its restricted range, New Guinea

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|311x311px|Cocos Island viewed from a helicopter The Cocos finch is exclusively found in Cocos Island (which takes up a 1,997 km2 area), 580 km southeast from Costa Rica. Cocos finches are not known to have traveled outside of Cocos Island, making its habitat and reproduction range to only be of the entire island. The habitat of the Cocos finch is mostly within hibiscus thickets, closed-canopy forest, and woodland forest. The climate within Cocos Island is described to be a typical rainforest climate with high humidity and chances for rainfall

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It occurs mainly in rainforest but is also sometimes found in drier woodland and scrub. Outside of Australia they may be found in southern New Guinea, where they are probably underreported and winter migrants.{{cite journal|last1=Bell|first1=H

birds.evidence: It inhabits rainforest, secondary forests, and plantations at elevations of up to , although it is more common at lower elevations

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It inhabits lowland rainforests, secondary forests, cleared areas and gardens

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Mindanao hornbill (Penelopides affinis), also known as the Mindanao tarictic hornbill, is a medium-small species of hornbill found in the canopy of rainforests on Mindanao, Dinagat, Siargao and Basilan in the southern Philippines

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: jobiensis]] The dove is found in New Guinea, the Aru Islands and western Papuan islands where it inhabits lowland rainforest, secondary forest and mangroves

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It can be found in tropical rainforest and is locally common in its range. Within Australia it has two isolated populations, both located in far north Queensland

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The king bird-of-paradise is distributed throughout the majority of lowland New Guinea mainland, and on the surrounding islands, including Aru, Salawati, Missol, and Yapen, inhabiting mostly lowland rainforests, gallery forests, forest edges, and disturbed and tall secondary forests

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It can commonly be found in tropical regions, subtropical rainforest, scrub, watercourses and street trees

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It mainly occurs in the interior and edges of pre-montane rainforest and cloudforest, but is also found in scrub, clearings, secondary forest, and coffee plantations

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|234x234px|Ranomafana National Park The velvet asity is endemic to Madagascar and is distributed along the eastern side and northwestern corner of the island, between sea level and altitudes of 1,800 m. It is commonly found in Ranomafana National Park (southeastern Madagascar) and the mountainous rainforest of the Masola Peninsula (northwestern Madagascar). Velvet asities live in subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. More specifically, they inhabit primary and secondary rainforests, as well as forested areas bordering zones affected by human disturbance (adjacent degraded rainforests). The species tends to favor locations with an open understory, especially along valley sides and bottoms. It has been recorded at both low and mid-elevations, but is most commonly found between 600 and 1,400 m. It is uncommon below 400 m and above 1,600 m, and is absent from montane sclerophyllous forests

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The birds are found in subtropical and temperate rainforest and paperbarks, and occasionally in adjacent eucalypt forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The scarlet macaw inhabits primarily humid, lowland subtropical rain forests, open woodlands, river edges, and savannas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The species inhabits several non-rainforest landscapes including semi-deciduous, secondary, and gallery forests, cerrado, and caatinga

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in a wide variety of habitats, from shrublands and grasslands through eucalypt, sheoak and Acacia woodlands, to dense tropical rainforests. The bird is dependent on large, old eucalypts for nesting hollows, although the specific gums used vary in different parts of the country

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It inhabits the understory of lowland rainforest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Native flocks exist in Brazil; Colombia; French Guiana; Guyana; Suriname; and Venezuela, as well as the islands of the Netherlands Antilles, and Trinidad and Tobago. Flocks gather in wetlands and other marshy habitats, including mud flats, shoreline and rainforest. Outlying colonies have been identified in the coastal areas of the states of Espírito Santo, Rio de Janeiro, São Paulo (for example in the Santos-Cubatão mangroves of the Baixada Santista district), Paraná and Santa Catarina. In recent years, bird colonies can be seen as far south as in the coastal areas of Joinville and the island of São Francisco do Sul. The highest concentrations are found in the Llanos region of western Venezuela and eastern Colombia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The species inhabits the understory and edges of a variety of landscapes including rainforest, tall secondary forest, humid semi-deciduous forest, cloudforest, and gallery forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It usually inhabits tropical lowland rainforests in the upper (emergent) canopy layer

birds.evidence: caurensis Bolívar and Amazonas states in southeastern Venezuela Specimens of this species labeled as from Grenada are believed to have been actually collected on Tobago, and "the occurrence of the species in the Lesser Antilles is doubtful." The copper-rumped hummingbird inhabits a wide variety of forest types including gallery forest, cloudforest, rainforest, and secondary forest

birds.evidence: The pheasant pigeon is found in the primary rainforests of New Guinea and nearby islands

birds.evidence: The species inhabits the understory of rainforest (especially it edges), secondary forest, and more open landscapes such as plantations and swamp forest

links.evidence: The Luzon scops owl is known to be found in rainforest, montane forest, and tropical pine forest habitats at altitudes generally ranging anywhere from 350 to 1,800 m, but it may also be found at heights upwards of 2,200m. Although its habitat range across Luzon appears to be relatively large, it is noted to be uncommon and has a patchy distribution that corresponds to its preferred, highland habitats. It has been recorded primarily in northern Luzon provinces such as Ilocos Norte, Benguet, Cagayan, Isabela, Nueva Ecija, or near Manila in Bulacan and Quezon

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|311x311px|Cocos Island viewed from a helicopter The Cocos finch is exclusively found in Cocos Island (which takes up a 1,997 km2 area), 580 km southeast from Costa Rica. Cocos finches are not known to have traveled outside of Cocos Island, making its habitat and reproduction range to only be of the entire island. The habitat of the Cocos finch is mostly within hibiscus thickets, closed-canopy forest, and woodland forest. The climate within Cocos Island is described to be a typical rainforest climate with high humidity and chances for rainfall

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|234x234px|Ranomafana National Park The velvet asity is endemic to Madagascar and is distributed along the eastern side and northwestern corner of the island, between sea level and altitudes of 1,800 m. It is commonly found in Ranomafana National Park (southeastern Madagascar) and the mountainous rainforest of the Masola Peninsula (northwestern Madagascar). Velvet asities live in subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. More specifically, they inhabit primary and secondary rainforests, as well as forested areas bordering zones affected by human disturbance (adjacent degraded rainforests). The species tends to favor locations with an open understory, especially along valley sides and bottoms. It has been recorded at both low and mid-elevations, but is most commonly found between 600 and 1,400 m. It is uncommon below 400 m and above 1,600 m, and is absent from montane sclerophyllous forests

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Native flocks exist in Brazil; Colombia; French Guiana; Guyana; Suriname; and Venezuela, as well as the islands of the Netherlands Antilles, and Trinidad and Tobago. Flocks gather in wetlands and other marshy habitats, including mud flats, shoreline and rainforest. Outlying colonies have been identified in the coastal areas of the states of Espírito Santo, Rio de Janeiro, São Paulo (for example in the Santos-Cubatão mangroves of the Baixada Santista district), Paraná and Santa Catarina. In recent years, bird colonies can be seen as far south as in the coastal areas of Joinville and the island of São Francisco do Sul. The highest concentrations are found in the Llanos region of western Venezuela and eastern Colombia
